sections.paragraph:
ὠφελέω [ὄφελος]

—1. ‘engage in activity that brings about someth. good above and beyond that which existed earlier’

—a. help, assist Mt 15:5; 16:26; Hb 4:2; μηδὲν ὠφεληϑεῖσα without being helped at all = without getting any better Mk 5:26; cause to benefit 1 Cor 14:6; be of advantage (to), be of benefit (to) Mk 8:36; Lk 9:25; 1 Cor 13:3; Gal 5:2; Hb 13:9.

—b. be of value Ro 2:25; ἡ σάρξ οὐκ ὠφελεῖ οὐδεν the flesh is totally useless J 6:63.

—2. ‘be successful in an activity’, accomplish, w. neg. = get nowhere Mt 27:24; J 12:19.
